hero.eyebrow: A PERSONAL MONOGRAPH ON APPLIED AI SYSTEMS
hero.name: Nguyễn Lê Quốc Bảo
hero.role: AI systems engineer, backend architect, and repository-based builder
hero.lead: This folio records applied research directions, public repositories, and technical responsibilities across systems that connect data, models, APIs, and deployment practice.

about.title: Preface
about.body: The portfolio is concerned with systems thinking: how an AI idea becomes service boundaries, data flows, repository structure, validation, and a deployment path. It is arranged as a technical notebook so that the reader can follow the work without decorative distraction.
highlights: Public repositories are indexed from ambrouse and baolnq-ai.
Case studies emphasize responsibility, implementation method, and operational structure.
The writing is intentionally restrained: clear, verifiable, and tied to observable engineering work.

capabilities.title: Technical studies
capabilities.lead: Capabilities are grouped by system problem rather than by isolated technology names.

items.title: Retrieval and knowledge systems
items.description: Structuring ingestion, parsing, embedding, indexing, retrieval, and generation for applications that depend on internal knowledge.

items.title: Backend architecture
items.description: Designing APIs, service layers, asynchronous workers, data stores, and integration boundaries for maintainable systems.

items.title: Edge AI inference
items.description: Building pipelines for camera streams, inference, state transitions, and runtime constraints on edge devices.

items.title: Operational discipline
items.description: Connecting CI/CD, configuration, observability, backup flows, and documentation to the same verifiable deployment path.

cases.title: Five architectural records
cases.lead: The selected records emphasize systems that show clearer structure: pipeline boundaries, orchestration logic, integration surfaces, and enough repository scale to be read as technical studies rather than isolated exercises.

items.label: CASE I
items.title: Provider-side agentic commerce blueprint
items.summary: A TypeScript blueprint that treats commerce as an interface problem between provider catalogues, agent workflows, and verifiable integration contracts.
bullets: Frames provider operations as explicit surfaces rather than informal API calls.
Separates commerce intent, service boundary, and implementation detail into a reviewable repository structure.
Useful as a study in agent-ready business workflow modelling and integration discipline.

items.label: CASE II
items.title: Multi-agent intelligent warehouse study
items.summary: A warehouse-oriented repository that organizes multi-agent reasoning around operational coordination, inventory movement, and applied AI infrastructure signals.
bullets: Uses the warehouse domain to make agent coordination concrete and auditable.
Combines operations logic with applied AI assumptions instead of presenting agents as a generic chat layer.
Reads as a systems blueprint because it links workflow structure, domain entities, and execution constraints.

items.label: CASE III
items.title: Browser automation agent pipeline
items.summary: A Python automation repository that examines how browser tasks can be mediated by model-assisted control while preserving a traceable execution path.
bullets: Treats browser work as a pipeline of observation, planning, action, and verification.
The value lies in operational flow and tool orchestration rather than a single UI screen.
Suitable for studying agent control loops, automation safety boundaries, and practical task execution.

items.label: CASE IV
items.title: Online note system with separated frontend and backend
items.summary: A two-repository application study that separates interface concerns from backend persistence and service responsibilities.
bullets: Demonstrates the discipline of separating client presentation from backend data ownership.
Provides a useful archive for reading application structure across frontend and backend repositories.
Shows the transition from feature implementation toward maintainable service boundaries.

items.label: CASE V
items.title: Fall-detection vision pipeline
items.summary: A computer-vision repository focused on applied detection logic, model output interpretation, and scenario-specific visual inference.
bullets: Grounds AI work in a concrete safety-oriented recognition problem.
Organizes model inference around observable events rather than abstract benchmark results.
Useful as a record of edge-oriented perception, dataset interpretation, and application-facing AI behaviour.

projects.title: Repository catalogue
projects.lead: Public repositories are filtered for practical value: live work opens directly, research work keeps the technical context, and implementation records stay easy to scan.
projects.search: Search by name, language, or keyword
projects.empty: No repository records match the current criteria.
projects.summary: public repositories grouped for demos, research notes, backend work and frontend implementations
projects.featuredLead: Compact records with stronger system, research or portfolio relevance.

contact.title: Work contact
contact.lead: For AI prototypes, backend/API work, deployment fixes, or repository review, send the goal, repository link, and current state. That keeps the conversation practical and easy to act on.
